title: Sentinel Shield operation lock
description: Transaction marker written to <target>/.sentinel-shield/operation-lock.json while a mutating operation (install/sync/migration/bootstrap) is in flight. Removed on successful commit; a lock left behind by an ungraceful kill is DETECTED on the next mutating run and recovered with --recover. Path values (target, snapshot_dir) are UNTRUSTED on read and MUST be re-validated for canonical containment before any use.

schema_version.description: Operation-lock schema version. Fixed at '1' for this contract.

operation.description: Mutating operation that owns the lock.

target.description: Canonical absolute path of the consuming project the operation is mutating. Re-validated against the current canonical target before any recovery.

started_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the operation acquired the lock.

pid.description: PID of the process that acquired the lock. A raw PID can be reused; ownership is additionally bound by 'token' and 'pid_start'. UNTRUSTED for liveness on its own.

snapshot_dir.description: Canonical path to the per-operation snapshot directory, which MUST live under <target>/.sentinel-shield/.txn-*. Re-validated for containment (no '..') before any restore.

state.description: Explicit state-machine position. Valid transitions only (see scripts/lib/transaction.sh _tx_state_transition_ok): initializing->active; active->validating|committing|rolling-back|completed; validating->committing|rolling-back; committing->completed; rolling-back->completed|rollback-incomplete; rollback-incomplete->rolling-back. 'completed' is durably recorded BEFORE the lock is removed, so a crash mid-finalise leaves a 'completed' marker the next run treats as already-finished (never rolled back). 'rollback-incomplete' retains the lock for manual recovery. An impossible transition is rejected.

token.description: PID-independent ownership token minted at acquisition (from /dev/urandom where available). Uniquely identifies THIS operation's ownership of the lock even if the PID is later reused by an unrelated process.

hostname.description: Host that acquired the lock. A lock whose hostname differs from the current host is never mistaken for a live process locally.

host_id.description: Stable machine identifier (Linux machine-id where available, else hostname). Distinguishes two hosts that share a hostname.

pid_start.description: Process-start identity of the owning PID (Linux /proc starttime, or BSD/macOS ps lstart). Empty where the platform exposes neither. Defeats PID reuse: a recycled PID whose start identity differs from this value is classified stale, not live.

engine_version.description: Sentinel Shield engine version that wrote the lock (audit).

lock_dir.description: Path to the atomic mutual-exclusion directory (<target>/.sentinel-shield/operation-lock.d) whose single mkdir grants ownership. Recorded for audit; the on-disk directory is authoritative.
